title: Ubuntu Linux
description: Complete command reference from beginner to advanced

commands.name: ls — List Directory
rows: Shows all files with permissions, size, owner

commands.name: mkdir — Make Directory
rows: Creates nested structure with file in one command

commands.name: rm — Remove Files/Dirs
rows: Asks before descending and deleting each item

commands.name: cp — Copy
rows: Creates b.txt as copy of f.txt

commands.name: Additional Utilities
rows: Shows directory tree visually (install: sudo apt install tree)
Adds text to end of file

commands.name: less — Scroll Through Files
rows: Like tail -f, Ctrl+C to stop, Shift+F resume
Jumps to first occurrence of pattern

commands.name: more — Scroll (Basic)
rows: Scroll one line at a time

commands.name: find — Search Files
rows: Pipe to wc for line count

commands.name: echo — Display & Append
rows: Adds one line to end of file

commands.name: chown — Change Owner/Group
rows: Only if current owner is root

commands.name: Special Permissions (SUID, SGID, Sticky)
rows: Runs as file owner, not the user executing
New files in folder inherit folder's group
Only file owners can delete own files
ls -ld myfolder (shows 's' in group execute: rws)
s instead of x means SGID is active

commands.name: id / groups / getent — Identity
rows: Lists all groups you belong to
Lists all users in sudo group

commands.name: Key System Files
rows: Default files for new user home dirs
Default UID ranges, password aging, etc.

commands.name: useradd — Create Users (Low-level)
rows: sudo useradd -m -d /custom/home -s /bin/bash -u 1500 -g devgroup -G sudo,docker -c "John Dev" dev2
-M = do NOT create home, -e = expiry date YYYY-MM-DD

commands.name: adduser — Create Users (High-level)
rows: Creates home, sets shell, asks password, full name

commands.name: passwd — Password Management
rows: Admin can set any user's password
Expires password, user must change on next login
Shows if user is locked/unlocked, last change

commands.name: chage — Password Aging
rows: Shows last change, expiry, warn days
Fully disable password and account expiration

commands.name: userdel — Delete Users
rows: Removes user entry, leaves home dir
Removes user, home dir, and mail spool

commands.name: System Users vs Normal Users
rows: Used for services: nginx, mysql, postgres
Normal user: UID >= 1000, has home, can login
ps aux | grep nginx (shows www-data user)
nginx runs as www-data, not root

commands.name: apt update & upgrade
rows: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y
Update then upgrade in one line

commands.name: apt install — Install Packages
rows: sudo apt install nginx curl git vim -y

commands.name: apt remove/purge — Remove Packages
rows: Also removes config of orphaned packages

commands.name: apt search & inspect
rows: All files installed by the package
Configures any unpacked but unconfigured packages

commands.name: Repository Management
rows: echo "deb http://repo.url focal main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/custom.list

commands.name: APT Cache Management
rows: How much space apt cache uses

commands.name: APT Fresh Setup Workflow
commands.code:
# Fresh system setup
sudo apt update                         # 1. Sync repos
sudo apt upgrade -y                     # 2. Upgrade existing packages
sudo apt install nginx curl git -y      # 3. Install what you need
apt show nginx                          # 4. Verify package details
dpkg -L nginx                           # 5. See where files were installed
sudo systemctl start nginx              # 6. Start the service

# Later - clean uninstall
sudo apt purge nginx -y
sudo apt autoremove -y
sudo apt clean

commands.name: ps — Process Status (Full)
rows: Every process with user, CPU%, MEM%

commands.name: top — Interactive Monitor
rows: q=quit, P=sort CPU, M=sort memory, k=kill, u=filter user, 1=cores
top fields: us=user cpu, sy=system cpu, id=idle, wa=iowait

commands.name: Background Jobs
rows: & at end sends process to background
Keeps running after you close terminal

commands.name: NGINX — Web Server Setup
rows: Create symlink to enable a site

commands.name: ifconfig — Legacy Network Tool
rows: Legacy tool, may need: sudo apt install net-tools
ip vs ifconfig: ip addr = ifconfig, ip link set = ifconfig up/down, ip route = route -n, ip neigh = arp -n
ip is modern, ifconfig is deprecated

commands.name: ping — Test Connectivity
rows: Stress test, sends fast as possible
Understanding output: icmp_seq, ttl, time, packet loss%

commands.name: curl — Full HTTP Client
rows: Pipes API response to JSON formatter

commands.name: wget — Download Tool
rows: Download all URLs from a file
curl for API, wget for reliable downloads

commands.name: netstat — Legacy Socket Stats
rows: Same as ss -tulpn, install: sudo apt install net-tools

commands.name: DNS Tools
rows: Full DNS response with TTL, authority
Only the IP address, no extra info

commands.name: UFW — Firewall
rows: sudo ufw allow from 192.168.1.0/24 to any port 22

commands.name: tee — Split Output
rows: Shows on screen AND saves to file

commands.name: Scripting Patterns
rows: No spaces around =, use $ to read
for f in *.txt; do echo "$f"; done
for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do echo $i; done
while read line; do echo "$line"; done < file.txt
Exit with error if command fails

commands.name: Complete backup.sh Script
commands.code:
#!/bin/bash
# backup.sh - usage: ./backup.sh source destination
src="$1"
dst="$2"
date=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
archive="backup_$date.tar.gz"

if [ -z "$src" ] || [ -z "$dst" ]; then
    echo "Usage: $0 <source> <dest>"
    exit 1
fi

if [ ! -d "$src" ]; then
    echo "Error: Source not found"
    exit 1
fi

tar -czf "$dst/$archive" "$src" 2>/dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "Backup created: $dst/$archive"
else
    echo "Backup failed"
    exit 1
fi

commands.name: Pipe & Redirection
rows: Pass output of one command to another
cmd2 runs only if cmd1 succeeds
cmd2 runs only if cmd1 fails

commands.name: System Monitoring
rows: Size of each item in current dir

commands.name: Keyboard Shortcuts
rows: End of file, or exit shell
Move cursor to beginning of line
Move cursor to end of line

commands.name: Useful One-liners
commands.code:
# Delete all .log files
find . -name "*.log" -type f -delete

# Top 5 memory processes
ps aux --sort=-%mem | head -5

# Monitor port 80 every 2 seconds
watch -n 2 'ss -tuln | grep 80'

# Top 10 most frequent lines
sort file.txt | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10

# Find files >1GB
find / -xdev -size +1G 2>/dev/null

# Check disk space alert (over 80%)
df -h | awk '$5+0 > 80 {print $1, $5}'

# List all listening ports with PIDs
ss -tulnp

# Kill all node processes
killall node

# Download entire website
wget --mirror -p --convert-links https://example.com

# Public IP check
curl ifconfig.me
